metadata.name: Base image uses latest tag
metadata.description: Mutable latest tags reduce image reproducibility and make supply-chain review harder.
metadata.mitigation: Pin the base image to an explicit version and preferably to an immutable digest.
metadata.reference: Docker image best practices; OCI image immutability and supply chain guidance.

metadata.name: Base image tag without digest
metadata.description: Version tags can be retagged and do not fully guarantee the exact base image content.
metadata.mitigation: Pin the base image by digest in addition to the version tag.
metadata.reference: Dockerfile best practices for image pinning and reproducible builds.

metadata.name: Final stage in multi-stage misses COPY --from
metadata.description: A multi-stage build without artifact transfer to the final stage often indicates an incorrect or incomplete packaging flow.
metadata.mitigation: Copy only the required build artifacts from the builder stage into the final runtime stage.
metadata.reference: Docker multi-stage build guidance for minimal and reproducible runtime images.

metadata.name: Single-stage image contains build tooling
metadata.description: Keeping compilers or build toolchains in the runtime image increases attack surface and image size.
metadata.mitigation: Use a dedicated builder stage and ship a minimal final runtime image.
metadata.reference: Docker multi-stage build guidance; container hardening guidance for minimal runtime images.

metadata.name: Missing USER in final stage
metadata.description: Containers that do not set USER in the final stage usually run as root by default.
metadata.mitigation: Create a dedicated non-root user and set USER in the final stage.
metadata.reference: Least-privilege container runtime guidance; CIS Docker Benchmark recommendations.

metadata.name: USER is root or uid 0
metadata.description: Running the container process as root increases the impact of container escape and application compromise scenarios.
metadata.mitigation: Run the application under a non-root user with the minimal required permissions.
metadata.reference: Least-privilege guidance for containers; CIS Docker Benchmark user recommendations.

metadata.name: USER uid must be greater than 1000
metadata.description: Low UIDs are often reserved for system identities and may conflict with host or base image accounts.
metadata.mitigation: Use an application-specific non-root user with a dedicated UID above the system range.
metadata.reference: Container hardening practices for dedicated non-root runtime users.

metadata.name: Secret-like ENV key detected
metadata.description: Embedding secret-like values in image environment metadata risks disclosure through image inspection and downstream reuse.
metadata.mitigation: Remove secrets from ENV and provide them at runtime through dedicated secret management mechanisms.
metadata.reference: Docker secrets guidance; NIST container security guidance for secret handling.

metadata.name: Secret-like ARG key detected
metadata.description: Build arguments can leak into build history, logs, or cache metadata when used for sensitive data.
metadata.mitigation: Do not pass secrets through ARG; use secret mounts or external secret delivery instead.
metadata.reference: BuildKit secret handling guidance; secure build pipeline practices.

metadata.name: COPY may include secret file
metadata.description: Copying secret material into an image can expose credentials to every consumer of the built artifact.
metadata.mitigation: Exclude secret files from the build context and inject credentials only at runtime or build time through secret mounts.
metadata.reference: Docker build context hygiene and secret management best practices.

metadata.name: ADD may include secret file
metadata.description: Adding secret material into an image permanently embeds it in the resulting layers.
metadata.mitigation: Keep secret files out of the image and use dedicated secret injection mechanisms.
metadata.reference: Docker image hardening and secret management guidance.

metadata.name: curl/wget piped to shell
metadata.description: Streaming remote content directly into a shell bypasses integrity verification and makes execution auditing difficult.
metadata.mitigation: Download the artifact separately, verify checksum or signature, and only then execute it.
metadata.reference: Secure software delivery and artifact verification guidance; supply-chain hardening practices.

metadata.name: curl/wget without verification
metadata.description: Downloaded artifacts that are not verified before use can be replaced or tampered with in transit or at source.
metadata.mitigation: Verify checksums or signatures for downloaded artifacts before executing or unpacking them.
metadata.reference: Artifact integrity verification guidance; secure software supply-chain practices.

metadata.name: ADD URL without checksum
metadata.description: Using ADD with a remote URL fetches external content without an explicit integrity verification step.
metadata.mitigation: Download remote artifacts explicitly and verify their checksum or signature before use.
metadata.reference: Dockerfile ADD semantics; secure artifact retrieval guidance.

metadata.name: Prefer COPY over ADD for local files
metadata.description: ADD has broader semantics than COPY and can introduce unexpected behavior for local file transfers.
metadata.mitigation: Use COPY for local files and reserve ADD only for cases that require its special behavior.
metadata.reference: Dockerfile best practices recommending COPY for predictable file inclusion.

metadata.name: apt-get update is separate from install
metadata.description: Separating apt metadata refresh from package installation can produce stale cache use and non-reproducible builds.
metadata.mitigation: Combine apt-get update and apt-get install in the same RUN step.
metadata.reference: Dockerfile package manager best practices for Debian-based images.

metadata.name: apt upgrade detected
metadata.description: Full distribution upgrades inside an image can pull uncontrolled package changes and reduce build determinism.
metadata.mitigation: Install only the required packages and pin versions where appropriate instead of performing broad upgrades.
metadata.reference: Container image reproducibility guidance; package management best practices.

metadata.name: apt-get install without apt lists cleanup
metadata.description: Leaving apt package metadata in the image increases image size and retains unnecessary package-management state.
metadata.mitigation: Remove apt lists after installation in the same RUN step.
metadata.reference: Dockerfile best practices for minimizing image layers and package manager artifacts.

metadata.name: apk add without --no-cache
metadata.description: Installing packages without --no-cache leaves package index data in the image unnecessarily.
metadata.mitigation: Use apk add --no-cache or otherwise remove cached package metadata in the same layer.
metadata.reference: Alpine package manager guidance for compact container images.

metadata.name: Cache mount without id
metadata.description: Unnamed cache mounts are harder to manage consistently across builds and builders.
metadata.mitigation: Assign an explicit id to cache mounts to make cache usage deterministic and maintainable.
metadata.reference: BuildKit cache mount guidance.

metadata.name: Apt cache mount without sharing=locked
metadata.description: Shared apt cache mounts without locking can cause cache corruption or inconsistent concurrent package operations.
metadata.mitigation: Use sharing=locked for apt-related cache mounts.
metadata.reference: BuildKit cache mount recommendations for apt workloads.

metadata.name: Missing HEALTHCHECK in final stage
metadata.description: Without a HEALTHCHECK, orchestrators and operators have less visibility into runtime liveness and readiness issues.
metadata.mitigation: Define a meaningful HEALTHCHECK in the final runtime stage.
metadata.reference: Docker HEALTHCHECK documentation and container operability guidance.
